forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a480
+project: local-review
+title: 'Review: content-budgeted batching — prime overflows on large diffs (15/15 fail)'
+---
+## Symptom
+A `review sha` over a large/multi-commit range fails ALL fan-out tasks (e.g. 15/15), tripping the completeness guard (`check_review_completeness`, >50% failed) and returning an error. Small/single-commit reviews (what `/finish` runs as `HEAD~1..HEAD`) are fine. The engine's error text blames "saturated/unavailable agent queue" — that is a guess and is WRONG.
+
+## Root cause (verified by code + reproduction)
+The fan-out grain is the VALIDATOR, not (validator × file). All changed files are inlined ONCE into a single shared "primed prefix" (`fleet.rs::render_run_prime`), and each of ~M validators forks that prime and runs its whole rule-set over ALL files at once. So it is **M invocations, each crammed with every diff** — NOT N×M.
+
+There is **no aggregate size budget on the prime** (only a per-file cap, `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES = 32 KB`), and the code comments note file-batching was deliberately removed ("no longer a file-batching dimension"). Worse, the prime inlines the FULL current content of each changed file (up to 32 KB), not just the diff — so even a 6-line change to a 1000-line file ships the whole file.
+
+When the combined inlined content exceeds the review model's context window, the prime turn fails; per `fleet.rs` the per-task fallback (`render_fleet_prompt`) ALSO carries everything, so it fails too → every validator fails uniformly and immediately (not a slow timeout). Hard cliff, all-or-nothing.
+
+## Reproduction (this repo, post-rebuild engine)
+`attempted` is constant 15 regardless of file count — proof the grain is per-validator (M), not N×M:
+| Range | Files | Added code | Result |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `ad7ffced1` | 1 | ~6 | 15 attempted, 0 failed |
+| `eccd288dc` | 12 | ~158 | 15 attempted, 0 failed |
+| `725ab50cd` | 75 | ~54 (mostly deletions) | 15 attempted, 0 failed |
+| `eccd288dc^..HEAD` | 114 | ~974 | 15 attempted, **15 FAILED** |
+75 files PASSED but 114-with-974-insertions FAILED → it is aggregate inlined **content volume**, not file count.
+
+## Quantification
+The prime ≈ sum of full contents of all distinct changed files (≤32 KB each). The failing range had ~31 distinct .rs files, several large (pool.rs, scope.rs, synthesize.rs, ~1000-line test_support.rs). ~31 files × ~15–20 KB ≈ 0.5–0.6 MB ≈ ~150k tokens of prime alone — plus reply budget (~16k) + validator suffix → overflows even Claude Haiku's 200k window. On the 32k-context local Llama backend it overflows almost immediately.
+
+## Fix: re-introduce a content-budgeted batching dimension
+Group files into batches whose combined inlined content fits a prime budget; within a batch keep the shared-prime / fork-per-validator trick (cheap, cached); across batches parallelize and merge findings (synthesis already dedups by file:line). Batches = `ceil(total_content / budget)` → **1 batch for small diffs (today's fast path, unchanged), >1 for large** — turning the cliff into linear scaling. This also improves thoroughness (less attention dilution → better "report every instance").
+
+## Design decisions (see conversation — batch size discussion)
+- **Budget unit**: inlined content bytes/tokens, NOT file count (75-files-passed disproves count).
+- **Budget value**: backend-aware (derived from model context window, reserving reply + suffix + safety) vs a single fixed conservative constant. TBD with user.
+- **Per-file inline strategy**: keep whole-file inline (quality) vs diff+window only (much smaller prime). Interacts with batch size. TBD.
+- **Per-file floor**: a single file whose content exceeds the batch budget must fall back to bounded-slice + `read_file` spill so it never makes an unschedulable batch.
+- **Packing**: greedy bin-pack to minimize batch count (each batch = one prime decode).
+
+## Also
+- Replace the misleading "saturated/unavailable agent queue" wording in `check_review_completeness` (review_op.rs) — the real cause is usually prime/context overflow on a large diff. Consider surfacing "diff too large; was/should be batched" guidance.
+- Acceptance: a `review sha eccd288dc^..HEAD`-class range (114 files) completes with findings instead of 15/15 failing; small reviews unchanged (still 1 batch).
\ No newline at end of file

-| Range | Files | Added code | Result |
-|---|---|---|---|

The failing range had ~31 distinct .rs files, several large (pool.rs, scope.rs, synthesize.rs, ~1000-line test_support.rs). ~31 files × ~15–20 KB ≈ 0.5–0.6 MB ≈ ~150k tokens of prime alone — plus reply budget (~16k) + validator suffix → overflows even Claude Haiku's 200k window. On the 32k-context local Llama backend it overflows almost immediately.
-

Batches = `ceil(total_content / budget)` → **1 batch for small diffs (today's fast path, unchanged), >1 for large** — turning the cliff into linear scaling. This also improves thoroughness (less attention dilution → better "report every instance").
-
-## Design decisions (see conversation — batch size discussion)
-- **Budget unit**: inlined content bytes/tokens, NOT file count (75-files-passed disproves count).
-- **Budget value**: backend-aware (derived from model context window, reserving reply + suffix + safety) vs a single fixed conservative constant. TBD with user.
-- **Per-file inline strategy**: keep whole-file inline (quality) vs diff+window only (much smaller prime). Interacts with batch size. TBD.
-- **Per-file floor**: a single file whose content exceeds the batch budget must fall back to bounded-slice + `read_file` spill so it never makes an unschedulable batch.
-- **Packing**: greedy bin-pack to minimize batch count (each batch = one prime decode).
-
-## Also
-- Replace the misleading "saturated/unavailable agent queue" wording in `check_review_completeness` (review_op.rs) — the real cause is usually prime/context overflow on a large diff. Consider surfacing "diff too large; was/should be batched" guidance.
-- Acceptance: a `review sha eccd288dc^..HEAD`-class range (114 files) completes with findings instead of 15/15 failing; small reviews unchanged (still 1 batch).
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -12,9 +12,7 @@
 ## Root cause (verified by code + reproduction)
 The fan-out grain is the VALIDATOR, not (validator × file). All changed files are inlined ONCE into a single shared "primed prefix" (`fleet.rs::render_run_prime`), and each of ~M validators forks that prime and runs its whole rule-set over ALL files at once. So it is **M invocations, each crammed with every diff** — NOT N×M.

-There is **no aggregate size budget on the prime** (only a per-file cap, `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES = 32 KB`), and the code comments note file-batching was deliberately removed ("no longer a file-batching dimension"). Worse, the prime inlines the FULL current content of each changed file (up to 32 KB), not just the diff — so even a 6-line change to a 1000-line file ships the whole file.
-
-When the combined inlined content exceeds the review model's context window, the prime turn fails; per `fleet.rs` the per-task fallback (`render_fleet_prompt`) ALSO carries everything, so it fails too → every validator fails uniformly and immediately (not a slow timeout). Hard cliff, all-or-nothing.
+There is **no aggregate size budget on the prime** (only a per-file cap, `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES = 32 KB`), and file-batching was deliberately removed ("no longer a file-batching dimension"). The prime inlines the FULL current content of each changed file (≤32 KB), not just the diff. When the combined content exceeds the review model's context window the prime turn fails; the per-task fallback (`render_fleet_prompt`) ALSO carries everything and also fails → every validator fails uniformly and immediately (not a slow timeout). Hard cliff, all-or-nothing.

 ## Reproduction (this repo, post-rebuild engine)
 `attempted` is constant 15 regardless of file count — proof the grain is per-validator (M), not N×M:
@@ -26,19 +24,25 @@
 | `eccd288dc^..HEAD` | 114 | ~974 | 15 attempted, **15 FAILED** |
 75 files PASSED but 114-with-974-insertions FAILED → it is aggregate inlined **content volume**, not file count.

-## Quantification
-The prime ≈ sum of full contents of all distinct changed files (≤32 KB each). The failing range had ~31 distinct .rs files, several large (pool.rs, scope.rs, synthesize.rs, ~1000-line test_support.rs). ~31 files × ~15–20 KB ≈ 0.5–0.6 MB ≈ ~150k tokens of prime alone — plus reply budget (~16k) + validator suffix → overflows even Claude Haiku's 200k window. On the 32k-context local Llama backend it overflows almost immediately.
+## Decided design — content-budgeted batching at file granularity
+Rationale: cramming every diff into one context reviews POORLY even when it fits (attention dilution → misses instances, fights "report every instance"). So batches are kept deliberately small and is a tunable, NOT derived from the model context window.
+
+- **`batch_size` is a NEW parameter on the review tool**, default **32 KB (32768 bytes)**. Applies to every scope (`working`/`sha`/`file`).
+- **Whole-file granularity.** Greedily pack whole files into a batch until adding the next file's inlined content would exceed `batch_size`; then start a new batch. **NEVER split a file across batches** — a file is atomic.
+- **A single file whose inlined content exceeds `batch_size` is a HARD ERROR.** Do NOT degrade it to a bounded slice and do NOT spill to `read_file`. Error must name the file, its size, and the limit, and instruct: raise `batch_size` or narrow scope.
+- Each batch runs the full fan-out: its own shared prime over that batch's files, fork per validator. **Findings merged + deduped by file:line across batches** (synthesis already dedups by file:line).
+- Batches are independent → parallelize across the pool (respecting the pipeline gate / worker count). Total invocations = M validators × B batches, each bounded by `batch_size`.

-## Fix: re-introduce a content-budgeted batching dimension
-Group files into batches whose combined inlined content fits a prime budget; within a batch keep the shared-prime / fork-per-validator trick (cheap, cached); across batches parallelize and merge findings (synthesis already dedups by file:line). Batches = `ceil(total_content / budget)` → **1 batch for small diffs (today's fast path, unchanged), >1 for large** — turning the cliff into linear scaling. This also improves thoroughness (less attention dilution → better "report every instance").
+## Reconcile with existing per-file cap
+Today an oversized file silently degrades to a bounded slice via `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES = 32 KB` (+ `OVERSIZED_FILE_READ_NOTE`). The new rule REPLACES that silent degrade with an explicit error (fail loud, consistent with the rest of the engine). Default `batch_size` == that 32 KB cap, so default behavior: files ≤32 KB inline whole and pack into batches; the first file over 32 KB errors instead of being quietly trimmed. Remove/retarget the slice-degrade path accordingly.

-## Design decisions (see conversation — batch size discussion)
-- **Budget unit**: inlined content bytes/tokens, NOT file count (75-files-passed disproves count).
-- **Budget value**: backend-aware (derived from model context window, reserving reply + suffix + safety) vs a single fixed conservative constant. TBD with user.
-- **Per-file inline strategy**: keep whole-file inline (quality) vs diff+window only (much smaller prime). Interacts with batch size. TBD.
-- **Per-file floor**: a single file whose content exceeds the batch budget must fall back to bounded-slice + `read_file` spill so it never makes an unschedulable batch.
-- **Packing**: greedy bin-pack to minimize batch count (each batch = one prime decode).
+## Wiring
+- Review tool schema: add `batch_size` (bytes, default 32768) to the `review` op input; thread through `ReviewRequest` → scope/fleet.
+- Batching dimension lives where the work-list / prime is built (`scope.rs` work-list → `fleet.rs` prime), restoring a batching loop around the per-run prime.
+- Replace the misleading "saturated/unavailable agent queue" wording in `check_review_completeness` (review_op.rs) — distinguish real fan-out failure from "diff too large / file over batch_size".

-## Also
-- Replace the misleading "saturated/unavailable agent queue" wording in `check_review_completeness` (review_op.rs) — the real cause is usually prime/context overflow on a large diff. Consider surfacing "diff too large; was/should be batched" guidance.
-- Acceptance: a `review sha eccd288dc^..HEAD`-class range (114 files) completes with findings instead of 15/15 failing; small reviews unchanged (still 1 batch).
\ No newline at end of file
+## Acceptance
+- `review sha eccd288dc^..HEAD`-class range (114 files) completes with findings (batched), instead of 15/15 failing.
+- Small reviews unchanged (still 1 batch).
+- A single file > `batch_size` returns a clear error naming the file + size + limit; no file is ever split.
+- `batch_size` is settable per call and defaults to 32 KB.
\ No newline at end of file

 assignees:

+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kw4gdshhd76ahsw28jkdd8qy
+  text: |-
+    Picked up. Research done. As-built plan:
+
+    - batch_size carried on the existing engine config struct FleetConfig (already threaded run_review_over_agent -> run_pipeline_in_connection -> run_review). Default 32 KiB via manual Default impl. Exposed on ReviewRequest as Option<usize>; tool schema gains a `batch_size` Integer param on review file/working/sha.
+    - scope.rs: REMOVE the slice-degrade path (inline_or_slice/bounded_slice/hunk_window/dedup_sections/MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES/OVERSIZED_FILE_READ_NOTE) and the FileWork.inlined_full flag. Every file inlines its FULL source (source_slice = after-content; empty for a deletion).
+    - NEW pure fn scope::batch_work_list(&WorkList, batch_size) -> Result<Vec<WorkList>>: greedily packs whole distinct files (first-seen order) by source_slice byte length; a single file > batch_size is the HARD ERROR (names file+size+limit). Each batch WorkList = validators projected onto that batch's files (validators with no files in a batch dropped). This is the single oversize-error site.
+    - run_review: loop the existing run_fleet -> build_candidates -> verify_findings -> unpin machinery PER BATCH, accumulate VerifiedFindings + sum the fan-out tally, then synthesize once (synthesize already dedups by file:line). 1 batch == today's path exactly (prime reused across fan-out+verify), preserving the fast path.
+    - review_op::check_review_completeness: replace the "saturated/unavailable agent queue" guess wording.
+
+    TDD order: unit tests for batch_work_list (pack/never-split, oversize error, 1-batch small diff, validator projection) first, then the per-batch run_review loop via the drive.rs scripted-agent e2e harness (multi-file diff + small batch_size -> 2 batches merged), then tool wiring.

+  text: |-
+    Implementation landed and green. Left in `doing` for review.
+
+    DESIGN AS-BUILT:
+    - batch_size threaded on FleetConfig (pub batch_size + DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE=32*1024 + manual Default), the existing engine-config struct already wired run_review_over_agent -> run_pipeline_in_connection -> run_review. ReviewRequest gained `batch_size: Option<usize>`; tool reads it via new `usize_arg` helper; `BATCH_SIZE_PARAM` (Integer) added to review file/working/sha schemas. None -> default.
+    - scope.rs: REMOVED the slice-degrade path entirely (inline_or_slice/bounded_slice/hunk_window/dedup_sections/MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES/OVERSIZED_FILE_READ_NOTE) AND the FileWork.inlined_full flag. source_slice is now always the file's full after-content (empty for a deletion). render_file_block always frames "complete current contents".
+    - NEW pure `scope::batch_work_list(&WorkList, batch_size) -> Result<Vec<WorkList>, AvpError>`: greedily packs work.distinct_files() (first-seen order = the prime's file set) measuring each file by source_slice.len(); starts a new batch when current_bytes+size would exceed batch_size; a single file with size>batch_size is the HARD ERROR (names file + byte size + limit + "raise batch_size or narrow scope"). This is the SINGLE oversize-error site. `project_onto_files` projects each batch onto its file subset, dropping validators with no files in the batch, carrying change_purpose verbatim.
+    - run_review: loops batches; per batch run_fleet (own shared prime, fork per validator) -> build_candidates(batch, findings) -> verify_findings(.., batch prime session) -> unpin that batch's prime -> accumulate VerifiedFindings + sum (attempted, failed) tally. synthesize ONCE on the merged verified findings (its existing file:line dedup collapses cross-batch repeats). 1 batch == today's exact path (prime reused across fan-out AND verify), so the small-diff fast path is unchanged.
+    - review_op::check_review_completeness: replaced the "saturated/unavailable agent queue" guess with text that names a genuine per-task fan-out failure and explicitly says a large diff is now batched and a single file over batch_size fails fast with its own distinct error.
+
+    INVARIANTS HELD (verified by tests + adversarial double-check PASS): a file is never split across batches; a file > batch_size errors (not a slice, not a spill); per-batch prime pin lifecycle correct (unpinned per batch, Drop covers cancellation).
+
+    TESTS ADDED:
+    - scope.rs (pure): batch_work_list_packs_whole_files_within_the_byte_budget, _errors_on_a_single_file_over_the_budget, _small_diff_is_exactly_one_batch, _projects_each_validator_onto_its_batch_files, _keeps_a_shared_file_atomic_in_one_batch, _empty_work_yields_no_batches.
+    - drive.rs (real-pipeline e2e via scripted agent + seeded_two_file_dup_repo fixture, orthogonal embeddings so files don't cross-reference): review_batches_a_large_diff_and_merges_findings_across_batches — batch_size=250 splits two ~181-byte files into 2 batches; asserts BOTH src/lib.rs:3 and src/other.rs:3 render and counts.findings==2 (proves merge; tasks_attempted=2 confirms 2 fan-out batches).
+    - op_tool_helpers.rs: usize_arg_reads_unsigned_ints_only.
+    Replaced the two obsolete slice tests; updated FileWork test helpers (dropped inlined_full); removed the now-dead fallback_payload fleet test + added_entity helper.
+
+    TEST OUTPUT (real):
+    - cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::  -> 123 passed, 0 failed (incl. the 6 batch_work_list + 1 multi-batch e2e).
+    - cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools usize_arg review::  -> 22 passed, 0 failed.
+    - cargo nextest run -E 'rdeps(swissarmyhammer-validators)' --no-run -> compiles clean (fixed 2 extra ReviewRequest construction sites: tools tests.rs + agent review_real_model_e2e.rs).
+    - cargo fmt + cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-validators -p swissarmyhammer-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings -> clean.
+
+    NOTE / discovery: during the e2e test, the original shared dup-embedding fixture made every seeded file a mutual duplicate, so probe evidence cited every file in every batch and leaked file identifiers across batch prompts. Fixed by giving the two file-pairs orthogonal embeddings in the new fixture. Batching itself was correct throughout (tasks_attempted=2); only the test scripting needed the disjoint discriminator.
+
+    Files changed: crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/{scope.rs,fleet.rs,synthesize.rs,drive.rs,test_support.rs}; crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/op_tool_helpers.rs; crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/{mod.rs,review_op.rs,tests.rs}; crates/swissarmyhammer-agent/tests/review_real_model_e2e.rs.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-27T12:59:28.761491+00:00

+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-27 08:13)
+
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/tests.rs:724` — Magic number `150` configures a sleep duration in the concurrency probe without being named. The value configures test timing behavior; although the comment explains it widens the overlap window, the specific millisecond choice is unexplained. Extract `150` as a named test constant (e.g., `const CONCURRENCY_PROBE_SLEEP_MS: u64 = 150;`) with a comment documenting the empirical or derived basis for this duration.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/drive.rs:162` — Unexplained magic divisor `16` used to calculate keep-alive interval. The choice of dividing by 16 is not explained — whether it's frequency-based (16x per idle window), empirically tuned, or derived from another value is unclear. Extract `16` as a named constant with a comment explaining the divisor (e.g., `const KEEP_ALIVE_FREQUENCY_DIVISOR: u64 = 16; // 16x sampling within idle window`) and document the reasoning.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs:39` — All public items must have doc comments. This struct declaration has field-level documentation but no struct-level comment explaining its purpose. Add a struct-level doc comment above the struct definition, e.g. /// Configuration for content-budgeted batching in a review run.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs:39` — All public types with non-empty representation must implement Debug. This struct contains a usize field but has no Debug implementation. Add #[derive(Debug, Clone)] before pub struct FleetConfig to make the type debuggable and cloneable.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -66,44 +66,24 @@
 ## Root cause (verified by code + reproduction)
 The fan-out grain is the VALIDATOR, not (validator × file). All changed files are inlined ONCE into a single shared "primed prefix" (`fleet.rs::render_run_prime`), and each of ~M validators forks that prime and runs its whole rule-set over ALL files at once. So it is **M invocations, each crammed with every diff** — NOT N×M.

-There is **no aggregate size budget on the prime** (only a per-file cap, `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES = 32 KB`), and file-batching was deliberately removed ("no longer a file-batching dimension"). The prime inlines the FULL current content of each changed file (≤32 KB), not just the diff. When the combined content exceeds the review model's context window the prime turn fails; the per-task fallback (`render_fleet_prompt`) ALSO carries everything and also fails → every validator fails uniformly and immediately (not a slow timeout). Hard cliff, all-or-nothing.
+There is **no aggregate size budget on the prime**. When the combined content exceeds the review model's context window the prime turn fails; the per-task fallback ALSO carries everything and also fails → every validator fails uniformly and immediately. Hard cliff, all-or-nothing.

-## Reproduction (this repo, post-rebuild engine)
-`attempted` is constant 15 regardless of file count — proof the grain is per-validator (M), not N×M:
-| Range | Files | Added code | Result |
-|---|---|---|---|
-| `ad7ffced1` | 1 | ~6 | 15 attempted, 0 failed |
-| `eccd288dc` | 12 | ~158 | 15 attempted, 0 failed |
-| `725ab50cd` | 75 | ~54 (mostly deletions) | 15 attempted, 0 failed |
-| `eccd288dc^..HEAD` | 114 | ~974 | 15 attempted, **15 FAILED** |
-75 files PASSED but 114-with-974-insertions FAILED → it is aggregate inlined **content volume**, not file count.
+## Reproduction
+`attempted` is constant 15 regardless of file count — proof the grain is per-validator (M), not N×M. 75 files (mostly deletions) PASSED but 114-files/~974-insertions FAILED → it is aggregate inlined content volume, not file count.

 ## Decided design — content-budgeted batching at file granularity
-Rationale: cramming every diff into one context reviews POORLY even when it fits (attention dilution → misses instances, fights "report every instance"). So batches are kept deliberately small and is a tunable, NOT derived from the model context window.
-
-- **`batch_size` is a NEW parameter on the review tool**, default **32 KB (32768 bytes)**. Applies to every scope (`working`/`sha`/`file`).
-- **Whole-file granularity.** Greedily pack whole files into a batch until adding the next file's inlined content would exceed `batch_size`; then start a new batch. **NEVER split a file across batches** — a file is atomic.
-- **A single file whose inlined content exceeds `batch_size` is a HARD ERROR.** Do NOT degrade it to a bounded slice and do NOT spill to `read_file`. Error must name the file, its size, and the limit, and instruct: raise `batch_size` or narrow scope.
-- Each batch runs the full fan-out: its own shared prime over that batch's files, fork per validator. **Findings merged + deduped by file:line across batches** (synthesis already dedups by file:line).
-- Batches are independent → parallelize across the pool (respecting the pipeline gate / worker count). Total invocations = M validators × B batches, each bounded by `batch_size`.
-
-## Reconcile with existing per-file cap
-Today an oversized file silently degrades to a bounded slice via `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES = 32 KB` (+ `OVERSIZED_FILE_READ_NOTE`). The new rule REPLACES that silent degrade with an explicit error (fail loud, consistent with the rest of the engine). Default `batch_size` == that 32 KB cap, so default behavior: files ≤32 KB inline whole and pack into batches; the first file over 32 KB errors instead of being quietly trimmed. Remove/retarget the slice-degrade path accordingly.
-
-## Wiring
-- Review tool schema: add `batch_size` (bytes, default 32768) to the `review` op input; thread through `ReviewRequest` → scope/fleet.
-- Batching dimension lives where the work-list / prime is built (`scope.rs` work-list → `fleet.rs` prime), restoring a batching loop around the per-run prime.
-- Replace the misleading "saturated/unavailable agent queue" wording in `check_review_completeness` (review_op.rs) — distinguish real fan-out failure from "diff too large / file over batch_size".
+- **`batch_size` parameter on the review tool**, default **32 KB (32768 bytes)**. NOT model/context-window-driven — deliberately small (cramming reviews poorly even when it fits).
+- **Whole-file granularity.** Greedily pack whole files into a batch until adding the next would exceed `batch_size`; then start a new batch. **NEVER split a file across batches.**
+- **A single file whose inlined content exceeds `batch_size` is a HARD ERROR** (not sliced, not spilled) naming the file/size/limit.
+- Each batch runs the full fan-out (own prime, fork per validator); findings merged + deduped by file:line across batches.
+- Replaces the silent `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES` slice-degrade with the explicit error.

 ## Acceptance
-- `review sha eccd288dc^..HEAD`-class range (114 files) completes with findings (batched), instead of 15/15 failing.
-- Small reviews unchanged (still 1 batch).
-- A single file > `batch_size` returns a clear error naming the file + size + limit; no file is ever split.
-- `batch_size` is settable per call and defaults to 32 KB.
+- A 114-file-class range completes (batched) instead of 15/15 failing. Small reviews unchanged (1 batch). A single file > `batch_size` errors clearly; no file is ever split. `batch_size` settable per call, default 32 KB.

 ## Review Findings (2026-06-27 08:13)

reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -66,24 +66,44 @@
 ## Root cause (verified by code + reproduction)
 The fan-out grain is the VALIDATOR, not (validator × file). All changed files are inlined ONCE into a single shared "primed prefix" (`fleet.rs::render_run_prime`), and each of ~M validators forks that prime and runs its whole rule-set over ALL files at once. So it is **M invocations, each crammed with every diff** — NOT N×M.

-There is **no aggregate size budget on the prime**. When the combined content exceeds the review model's context window the prime turn fails; the per-task fallback ALSO carries everything and also fails → every validator fails uniformly and immediately. Hard cliff, all-or-nothing.
+There is **no aggregate size budget on the prime** (only a per-file cap, `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES = 32 KB`), and file-batching was deliberately removed ("no longer a file-batching dimension"). The prime inlines the FULL current content of each changed file (≤32 KB), not just the diff. When the combined content exceeds the review model's context window the prime turn fails; the per-task fallback (`render_fleet_prompt`) ALSO carries everything and also fails → every validator fails uniformly and immediately (not a slow timeout). Hard cliff, all-or-nothing.

-## Reproduction
-`attempted` is constant 15 regardless of file count — proof the grain is per-validator (M), not N×M. 75 files (mostly deletions) PASSED but 114-files/~974-insertions FAILED → it is aggregate inlined content volume, not file count.
+## Reproduction (this repo, post-rebuild engine)
+`attempted` is constant 15 regardless of file count — proof the grain is per-validator (M), not N×M:
+| Range | Files | Added code | Result |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `ad7ffced1` | 1 | ~6 | 15 attempted, 0 failed |
+| `eccd288dc` | 12 | ~158 | 15 attempted, 0 failed |
+| `725ab50cd` | 75 | ~54 (mostly deletions) | 15 attempted, 0 failed |
+| `eccd288dc^..HEAD` | 114 | ~974 | 15 attempted, **15 FAILED** |
+75 files PASSED but 114-with-974-insertions FAILED → it is aggregate inlined **content volume**, not file count.

 ## Decided design — content-budgeted batching at file granularity
-- **`batch_size` parameter on the review tool**, default **32 KB (32768 bytes)**. NOT model/context-window-driven — deliberately small (cramming reviews poorly even when it fits).
-- **Whole-file granularity.** Greedily pack whole files into a batch until adding the next would exceed `batch_size`; then start a new batch. **NEVER split a file across batches.**
-- **A single file whose inlined content exceeds `batch_size` is a HARD ERROR** (not sliced, not spilled) naming the file/size/limit.
-- Each batch runs the full fan-out (own prime, fork per validator); findings merged + deduped by file:line across batches.
-- Replaces the silent `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES` slice-degrade with the explicit error.
+Rationale: cramming every diff into one context reviews POORLY even when it fits (attention dilution → misses instances, fights "report every instance"). So batches are kept deliberately small and is a tunable, NOT derived from the model context window.
+
+- **`batch_size` is a NEW parameter on the review tool**, default **32 KB (32768 bytes)**. Applies to every scope (`working`/`sha`/`file`).
+- **Whole-file granularity.** Greedily pack whole files into a batch until adding the next file's inlined content would exceed `batch_size`; then start a new batch. **NEVER split a file across batches** — a file is atomic.
+- **A single file whose inlined content exceeds `batch_size` is a HARD ERROR.** Do NOT degrade it to a bounded slice and do NOT spill to `read_file`. Error must name the file, its size, and the limit, and instruct: raise `batch_size` or narrow scope.
+- Each batch runs the full fan-out: its own shared prime over that batch's files, fork per validator. **Findings merged + deduped by file:line across batches** (synthesis already dedups by file:line).
+- Batches are independent → parallelize across the pool (respecting the pipeline gate / worker count). Total invocations = M validators × B batches, each bounded by `batch_size`.
+
+## Reconcile with existing per-file cap
+Today an oversized file silently degrades to a bounded slice via `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES = 32 KB` (+ `OVERSIZED_FILE_READ_NOTE`). The new rule REPLACES that silent degrade with an explicit error (fail loud, consistent with the rest of the engine). Default `batch_size` == that 32 KB cap, so default behavior: files ≤32 KB inline whole and pack into batches; the first file over 32 KB errors instead of being quietly trimmed. Remove/retarget the slice-degrade path accordingly.
+
+## Wiring
+- Review tool schema: add `batch_size` (bytes, default 32768) to the `review` op input; thread through `ReviewRequest` → scope/fleet.
+- Batching dimension lives where the work-list / prime is built (`scope.rs` work-list → `fleet.rs` prime), restoring a batching loop around the per-run prime.
+- Replace the misleading "saturated/unavailable agent queue" wording in `check_review_completeness` (review_op.rs) — distinguish real fan-out failure from "diff too large / file over batch_size".

 ## Acceptance
-- A 114-file-class range completes (batched) instead of 15/15 failing. Small reviews unchanged (1 batch). A single file > `batch_size` errors clearly; no file is ever split. `batch_size` settable per call, default 32 KB.
+- `review sha eccd288dc^..HEAD`-class range (114 files) completes with findings (batched), instead of 15/15 failing.
+- Small reviews unchanged (still 1 batch).
+- A single file > `batch_size` returns a clear error naming the file + size + limit; no file is ever split.
+- `batch_size` is settable per call and defaults to 32 KB.

-- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/drive.rs:162` — Unexplained magic divisor `16` used to calculate keep-alive interval. Extract `16` as a named constant with a comment. → FIXED: `const KEEP_ALIVES_PER_IDLE_WINDOW: u64 = 16;`.
-- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs:39` — FleetConfig "has field-level documentation but no struct-level comment". → ALREADY PRESENT: FleetConfig has a multi-line `///` doc comment (fleet.rs:95-98); the finding mis-cited line 39 (a `}`). No change needed.
